user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to append time-stamped content to any document so that AI observations are added non-destructively to existing notes.
expected_behaviour:
POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/document/append/ — internal auth. Body: {kind (default daily), slug (optional; defaults to today for daily or kind for singletons), content (required)}. Validates kind+slug. Creates Document if missing. Appends '

### HH:MM — PersonaName
content
' to existing markdown. Returns {tenant_id, id, kind, slug, title, markdown, updated_at} HTTP 201.
inventory_notes: Different from daily-note append: always appends as a H3 timestamped block with persona name; no section-slug targeting.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to fetch all syncable documents as workspace-relative file paths so that the AI assistant can mirror the journal to the local filesystem.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/memory-sync/ — internal auth. Calls render_memory_files(tenant) from apps/orchestrator/memory_sync. Returns {tenant_id, files: {path: markdown_content}, count}. Files dict is workspace-relative paths. NOTE: memory_search is disabled fleet-wide; actual search routes through nbhd_journal_search → Postgres. This endpoint is a journal-of-record mirror only.
inventory_notes: memory_search (SQLite on Azure File Share) was disabled fleet-wide due to SMB lock/fsync corruption risk.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to report token usage for polling-mode turns so that billing and usage tracking are accurate.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/usage/report/ — internal auth. Body: {event_type (required, str), model_used (required, str), input_tokens (non-negative int), output_tokens (non-negative int)}. Calls record_usage(tenant, ...). Returns {status: 'ok'} HTTP 200. Returns 400 on missing required fields or invalid token counts (floats-not-integer, booleans, negative).
inventory_notes: Strict int parsing rejects booleans (bool is int subclass in Python) and float-with-decimal.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to report BYO provider errors so that the subscriber's AI Provider settings page shows an actionable error banner.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/byo/error/ — internal auth. Body: {provider (str, currently only 'anthropic'), reason (enum: billing/auth/auth_permanent/or other), message, model_used}. If reason not in {billing, auth, auth_permanent}, returns {status: 'ignored'} HTTP 200 (transient errors not surfaced). For actionable reasons, calls mark_credential_error which sets BYOCredential.status=error and last_error to a user-facing hint. If no matching credential found, returns {status: 'no_credential'} HTTP 200. Returns {status: 'ok', credential_id} on success. Always idempotent.
inventory_notes: Only 'anthropic' is a known provider. Unknown providers return 400. Non-actionable reasons (rate limits, transient overloads) are explicitly ignored.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to update the user's profile fields (timezone, display_name, language, location) after user confirms in conversation so that the platform stays in sync with user preferences.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/profile/ — internal auth. Allowed fields: timezone (validated via ZoneInfo), display_name (<=100 chars), language (<=10 chars), location_city (<=255 chars), location_lat+location_lon (float pair, validated ranges -90..90 and -180..180). At least one valid field must be provided (400 no_changes otherwise). Saves user fields. If timezone changed: updates all managed CronJob rows schedule.tz, bumps config, calls update_tenant_config. If location changed: bumps config, calls update_tenant_config.
dependencies: Postgres (User, CronJob models), OpenClaw container (config regeneration via apps/orchestrator)
inventory_notes: Timezone change propagates to all managed cron rows and triggers container config refresh so agent picks up new userTimezone.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to scan active goals, tasks, project docs, finance accounts, and fuel records for rows affected by a user's report so that the AI can suggest targeted updates rather than re-reading all data.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/reconcile/scan/?claim=<sentence> — internal auth. Tokenizes the claim (lowercase, strip stopwords, len>=3). Checks finance/fuel keyword triggers. Scores Goals (active, up to 50), Tasks (open/in-progress, up to 100), project Documents (kind=project, up to 50) by token substring match. Conditionally scores FinanceAccounts (if finance_triggered and tenant.finance_active) and Workouts/BodyWeightLog (if fuel_triggered and tenant.fuel_enabled). Each candidate includes kind, id, title, score, matched_tokens, current_state, and update_tools suggestion. Returns sorted by score, capped at ?limit (default 15, max 25).
dependencies: Postgres (Goal, Task, Document, FinanceAccount, Workout, BodyWeightLog models)
inventory_notes: Finance scan only runs if tenant.finance_active=True; fuel scan only if tenant.fuel_enabled=True. Claim is truncated to 500 chars. This is keyword-based (not semantic) matching.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to post a summary of a cron run so that the user's main chat session is informed about what the background cron just communicated.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/cron-phase2-summary/ — internal auth. Body: {summary (required, non-empty str), job_name (required)}. Computes a cron expression firing 2 minutes from now in tenant timezone (date-specific: m h d M * — fires once). Creates a _sync:<job_name> one-shot cron via cron.add gateway tool with sessionTarget=main, payload text = '[Sync — job_name] summary After noting this, run: cron remove _sync:<job_name>'. Returns {ok:true, sync_cron_name, fires_at} or 502 if gateway call fails.
inventory_notes: The cron expression encodes exact minute/hour/day/month, firing exactly once. The payload instructs the agent to self-remove via 'cron remove' after processing.

feature: Runtime typed cron create — pure reminder
user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to create a typed 'pure reminder' cron so that the user receives a scheduled fixed-text reminder without LLM generation.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/crons/pure_reminder/ — internal auth. Body: {name, schedule, text}. Creates typed CronJob via create_typed_cron with pattern=pure_reminder. Returns {tenant_id, cron: {id, name, pattern, schedule, managed, gateway_job_id}} HTTP 201. Returns 409 on name conflict (CronNameConflictError), 400 on validation errors or Pydantic schema mismatches.
dependencies: Postgres (CronJob model via apps/cron), OpenClaw container (gateway cron.add)

feature: Runtime typed cron create — quote user intent
user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to create a 'quote user intent' cron that re-states the user's own goal at schedule time so that the assistant grounds its message in what the user originally said.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/crons/quote_user_intent/ — internal auth. Body: {name, schedule, text, refresh_facts_via? (optional)}. Creates typed CronJob with pattern=quote_user_intent. Same 201/409/400 response contract as pure_reminder.

feature: Runtime typed cron create — domain summary
user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to create a 'domain summary' cron that queries a data source and renders a templated message so that regular reports are delivered to the user without ad-hoc LLM generation.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/crons/domain_summary/ — internal auth. Body: {name, schedule, query_tool, query_args (object), render_block}. Creates typed CronJob with pattern=domain_summary. Same 201/409/400 response contract.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container cron-enforcement plugin, I want to fetch the pattern context for a firing cron so that the correct validator and prompt injection are applied.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/crons/<cron_name>/pattern_context/ — internal auth. Calls fetch_cron_pattern_context(tenant_id, cron_name). Returns {pattern, typed_payload, name, prompt_injection} HTTP 200, or 404 {error: not_typed} if the cron has no typed pattern.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container cron-enforcement plugin, I want to know whether to inject the live-status grounding rule into a firing cron so that custom crons ground on live data while system crons avoid double-injection.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/crons/<cron_name>/grounding/ — internal auth. Returns {inject: false, rule: ''} for: crons starting with _SYSTEM_GENERATED_PREFIXES (_sync:/_fuel:), or crons whose payload.message contains CRON_PREAMBLE_MARKER (full preamble already baked). Returns {inject: true, rule: CRON_GROUNDING_RULE} for all other crons (typed patterns, user/freeform, legacy, agent-created, unknown).
inventory_notes: Cron rows not found in DB return inject=True (unknown/agent-created crons default to getting grounding).

user_story: As an OpenClaw container cron-enforcement plugin, I want to validate an outbound message against the firing cron's pattern contract so that typed crons deliver content that matches their declared template.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/crons/<cron_name>/validate_outbound/ — internal auth. Body: {content: str}. Returns {ok: bool, reason?: str, fallback_content?: str} HTTP 200 always (no 4xx for contract failures — returns ok=false with explanation instead). Returns 400 only if content is not a string.
inventory_notes: Validation failures return HTTP 200 with ok=false rather than 4xx — the enforcement plugin decides what to do (use fallback_content or block).

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to list and create workspaces for the tenant so that the AI assistant can organize work across multiple named contexts.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/workspaces/ — internal auth. Returns all Workspace rows ordered by -is_default, -last_used_at, name. Includes active_workspace_id and WORKSPACE_LIMIT. POST body: {name (required, max 60 chars), description}. Name must not start with reserved prefixes. If first workspace ever: auto-creates default workspace first. Enforces WORKSPACE_LIMIT (409 on breach). Generates unique slug. Creates workspace, embeds description via _embed_workspace_description, sets as active. Returns HTTP 201.
inventory_notes: Description is embedded via pgvector for future semantic workspace routing (not currently used for retrieval in this endpoint).

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to rename, update, or delete a workspace so that the user's workspace organization stays accurate.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/workspaces/<slug>/ — internal auth. Updates name (max 60, not reserved) and/or description (re-embeds description). PATCH with no valid fields still returns 200 with empty updated:[]. DELETE — prevents deletion of default workspace (409 cannot_delete_default). If deleting active workspace, falls back active_workspace to default. Hard-deletes the row. Returns {tenant_id, deleted_id, fell_back_to_default}.
inventory_notes: Default workspace cannot be deleted — prevents tenants from ending up with no workspace.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to switch the tenant's active workspace so that the AI assistant operates in the correct context for the current conversation.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/workspaces/switch/ — internal auth. Body: {slug (required)}. Finds workspace by slug (404 if not found). Sets tenant.active_workspace and saves. Updates workspace.last_used_at to now. Returns {tenant_id, workspace, previous_workspace_id}.
inventory_notes: switch/ URL must be registered before <slug>/ to avoid it being captured as a workspace slug.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to initiate Reddit OAuth via Composio on behalf of the user so that the AI assistant can guide the user through connecting their Reddit account.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/reddit/connect/ — internal auth. Body: {callback_url (required)}. Calls initiate_composio_connection to get a Composio redirect_url + connection_request_id. Returns {redirect_url, connection_request_id} HTTP 200. 400 on missing callback_url, 503 on IntegrationProviderConfigError, 500 on unexpected error.
inventory_notes: Connection_request_id is NOT cached here (unlike the subscriber flow via OAuthAuthorizeView); the agent must pass it to the complete endpoint directly.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to complete the Reddit OAuth flow by polling Composio until active so that the connection is confirmed and persisted.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/reddit/complete/ — internal auth. Body: {connection_request_id (required)}. Calls complete_composio_connection which polls Composio for up to 30 seconds for status=ACTIVE, then upserts Integration row. Returns {connected: true, provider_email} HTTP 200. 400 on missing connection_request_id. 409/503 on specific integration errors. 500 on unexpected error.
inventory_notes: 30-second synchronous poll on the Django request thread. If user doesn't complete OAuth within 30s, connection fails.
